reasoning: Formal declarations of war are effectively obsolete in modern international relations, as states seek to avoid the legal and diplomatic consequences under international law (e.g., the UN Charter). This is heavily reinforced by the recent events of May 2025, when India and Pakistan engaged in a major, 4-day armed conflict involving airstrikes and targeted missile attacks. Despite this intense use of military force—the most severe since 1971—neither side issued a formal declaration of war. More recently, in November 2025, explosive blasts in New Delhi and Islamabad have heightened tensions and spurred accusations, but have not resulted in any formal declarations of war. With only 38 days remaining in 2025 and overwhelming precedent that these countries will engage in significant hostilities *without* formally declaring war, the probability of a formal declaration occurring before December 31, 2025, is practically zero. Therefore, I am predicting the lowest allowed probability of 2%.
